Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the milestone brief against the actual overlay surface in `src/shared/components/ui/*` and the plan's proposed migration list. flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the user requirement that z-index ownership stop being hardcoded across files, not just inside wrappers. Current call sites still override overlay layering directly through props and classes, for example `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` uses `overlayClassName="!z-[100012]"` and `zIndexBase={UI_Z_LAYERS.LIGHTBOX_MODAL + 2}`, `sharedSections.tsx` passes `className="z-[100001]"` to `SelectContent`, and many `TooltipContent` callers in the lightbox and variant selector pass `z-[100001]` or `z-[100010]`; the plan does not make those call-site overrides a first-class cleanup target, so it only partially satisfies the approved requirement that layering flow through the central stack.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the current nested-modal path in `LoraSelectorModal.tsx`. That caller depends on `DialogContent`'s `zIndexBase` and `overlayClassName` escape hatches to sit above the lightbox, and it closes itself only when `getTopDialogElement() === contentRef.current`; the plan says those z-index escape hatches should probably be removed and wrapper-local layering should come from the new stack, but it never explains how this specific caller will keep its above-lightbox ordering or how the top-dialog check will continue to work once the rendered topmost element may no longer be the same content node reference.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the existing lightbox interaction logic in `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `uiLayers.ts`. Those paths currently gate outside-click and Escape behavior by scanning DOM elements marked with `data-dialog-backdrop`, `data-dialog-content`, and `data-lightbox-popup` and comparing computed z-index values; if M2 introduces `overlayStack` as the new source of truth but leaves these DOM-scanning guards in place, the app will have two competing overlay-order mechanisms, which is a correctness risk rather than just an implementation detail.
